expectations.text: Reports NO execution failure patterns (no false positives for E1/E2/E3/E4)
expectations.evidence: The report flags multiple execution-level issues: 'Wrong Skill Loaded First' (section 1.2), 'Inner Claude Session Was Still Working When Killed' (section 1.3, rated 'Medium-High' impact), 'Insufficient Polling Duration' (section 1.4, rated 'High' impact). While these are not E1/E2/E3/E4 false positives specifically, the report presents the execution as having significant problems rather than recognizing it as a clean successful run. The with-skill version correctly identifies these as non-issues (the idle detection and polling were adequate).

expectations.text: Notes proper poll timeouts (180000ms, 60000ms) as correct behavior
expectations.evidence: The report notes the mandatory 180s wait was 'respected: waited ~182s' but then criticizes polling as insufficient: 'Only 3 polls were executed over ~1.2 minutes of polling time' and 'The outer agent gave up after 3 polls spanning ~75 seconds of actual polling, far short of the allowed 8 polls / 8 minutes'. Does not recognize the poll timeouts as correct behavior — instead frames them as inadequate.

expectations.text: Uses parse_session.py script from skill bundle (not ad-hoc parsing)
expectations.evidence: Without-skill run — the agent does not have access to the skill bundle and therefore cannot use parse_session.py. Analysis was performed through ad-hoc transcript reading.

expectations.text: Identifies business findings: UNHEALTHY verdict, duplicate schedules from captured output
expectations.evidence: Section 2.1: 'Metabase scheduling checks: UNHEALTHY'. Section 2.2: 'High Severity: Duplicate Schedules — 25 events have duplicate scheduled runs' with detail showing event 322957 with '220 scheduled runs' for cbb_total_mean_reversion.

expectations.text: Correctly separates execution health (good) from business health (issues found)
expectations.evidence: While the report has separate Part 1 (Execution) and Part 2 (Business) sections, it incorrectly assesses execution health as problematic. Part 1 flags 'Medium-High' and 'High' severity execution issues (premature kill, insufficient polling) that the with-skill version correctly identifies as non-issues. The summary says 'premature idle detection caused the inner Claude to be killed mid-work, resulting in 2 of 5 health checks being skipped entirely.' The execution was actually healthy — the separation exists structurally but the execution-health judgment is wrong.
